method: For each of the 82 Todo-open issues in the W0-R1 snapshot, the R5 tentacle (a) assigned a family token derived from the title prefix (WP-19, WP-20, WP-21, WP-22, WP-23, VERIF-OPS, LF, DM, HC, SM, MACOS-WBS, CTRL, MODEL, QA, TEST, CI, SEC, REL, LINUX-WBS, PROC, XPLAT, PARITY, UX, I18N, CFG, STD, SUPERTONIC, QA8); (b) consumed the files_touched_hint values from the snapshot row enriched with the R4 audit's per-issue files_touched_hint (which are the more precise per-issue paths produced by reading every LOW-confidence issue body); (c) classified each hint as either a concrete repo path (file that exists today OR an explicit new file with a clearly named target), a broad glob (e.g., src/audio/**), or an evidence path (verification-evidence/**); (d) gave every concrete src path a single owning family with an explicit serialization rule when more than one issue/family touches it; (e) treated broad globs as planning-stage touchpoints and did NOT use them to establish ownership; (f) treated verification-evidence/** paths as additive per-issue evidence that does not collide.
principles: Concrete src/** paths must have exactly one owning family.
Where multiple issues inside the same family touch a concrete src path, ownership is family-level and the issue-level order is the family's internal dependency chain.
Where multiple families touch the same concrete src path, an explicit cross-family owner and serialization rule MUST be recorded; otherwise the path is BLOCKED.
Broad globs (src/audio/**, src/providers/**, src/**, etc.) are not ownership claims — they are touchpoint hints used only to flag which families need to coordinate.
Cargo.toml and .github/workflows/*.yml are merge-friendly manifest files; serialization is by dependency chain (additive edits) rather than exclusive ownership.
verification-evidence/** is per-issue evidence storage and never collides; each issue owns its own subdirectory or file.

WP-20.label: Post-v1 gRPC Streaming STT (epic, deferred)
WP-20.notes: Post-v1 epic with no current concrete WBS children on the board; planning-stage only.

WP-22.label: Azure Provider Integration (epic, deferred)
WP-22.notes: Future provider; will own a new src/providers/azure/ subdirectory when WBS children are filed. No collision today.

WP-23.label: Ollama Local Translation (epic, deferred)
WP-23.notes: Future provider; will own a new src/providers/ollama/ subdirectory when WBS children are filed. No collision today.

VERIF-OPS.label: Layer-5 reviewer recruiting (human-only)
VERIF-OPS.notes: Per W0-R4: 'Automation cannot complete this issue.' Owns the acceptance-log path only.

LF.label: Local-first defaults epic
LF.notes: Epic; concrete ownership will be determined by future LF-NN WBS children.

DM.label: Dual-provider transcript mode
DM.notes: Only DM-08 (#384) is on the board today; it is documentation/config-examples scoped (docs/** and config schema notes) — no current src/** ownership claim.

HC.label: Hot config without restart (epic)
HC.notes: Epic; will be sub-decomposed by HC-NN WBS children before claiming concrete config files.

shared_within_family.rationale: MACOS-02 lands first per internal chain; MACOS-03 extends the device-probe enumerator.
MACOS-02 registers the CoreAudio capture variant; MACOS-03 adds the ScreenCaptureKit variant.
CTRL-01 owns the gain hook in the playback sink.
CTRL-01 introduces volume gauges/hotkeys; CTRL-02 layers the voice picker on top after CTRL-01's TUI surface lands.
CTRL-02 sole CTRL-family toucher of Google TTS provider code (voice catalog).

MODEL.label: Backend selection and macOS local-model validation

shared_within_family.rationale: MODEL-01 defines the local provider contract; MODEL-02 validates macOS-specific build/runtime under that contract.
Same as above for the MT subtree.

QA.label: QA plan and traceability (ISO 25010/29119)
QA.notes: QA-01 and QA-02 own QA artifacts under verification-evidence/qa/ and docs/04-verification-plan.md additions. No src/** ownership.

TEST.label: Deterministic simulation harness

concrete_src_ownership.note: Snapshot lists this as TEST-01's input; treat as owned for harness changes.

TEST.notes: TEST-01 also extends tests/fixtures/, tests/contract/, tests/pty/, tests/soak/; tests/** is outside src/** scope and treated as additive per-issue ownership.

LINUX-WBS.label: Linux support roadmap
LINUX-WBS.notes: Linux capture WBS children (LINUX-01..05) reuse the audio HAL pattern; concrete file ownership will land as new src/audio/pipewire_capture.rs / src/audio/pulse_capture.rs etc. once LINUX-01 (#468) spike resolves. Treated as planning-stage today.

XPLAT.label: Cross-platform core and audio HAL architecture
XPLAT.notes: Architectural ADR; no exclusive src/** ownership until child issues materialize.

UX.label: Adaptive TUI and shortcut display
UX.notes: Both UX-01 and UX-02 touch src/tui/** broadly; specific file ownership inside src/tui/ (frame_pacer.rs, onboarding.rs, mod.rs) is not yet split. Planning-stage.

STD.label: Strict engineering standards and module budgets
STD.notes: STD-02 refactors oversized modules — cuts across many src/** files but does not establish exclusive ownership; serialization rule below.

concrete_src_ownership.rationale: SUPERTONIC-08 (#493) creates the local_mt/Supertonic provider subtree per the snapshot hint files_touched_hint; subsequent SUPERTONIC issues edit inside it.

shared_within_family.rationale: All SUPERTONIC issues land inside the local_mt subtree; serialization is the family chain. 485/486/487/488/489/490 are spike/license/benchmark and produce evidence under verification-evidence/, not src/, code edits.

QA8.label: 8-hour soak / 60fps QA epic
QA8.notes: Touches src/metrics/** broadly; concrete ownership of individual metrics files (cost.rs, latency.rs, loss.rs, memory_guard.rs, network.rs, process.rs, snapshot.rs) is not split per issue. Serialization is by QA8 internal chain. Evidence is per-issue under verification-evidence/qa8/<issue>/.

issues.notes: DM-08 is documentation/config-examples scoped per title.

Conflict with UX-01 (#479) on src/tui/frame_pacer.rs: UX-01 owns the file for adaptive layout; QA8-06 reads its existing telemetry. Resolution: QA8-06 reads but does not modify; if write is needed, QA8-06 rebases on UX-01.

shared_paths_in.rule: QA8-09 extends TEST-01's deterministic file source for cross-platform parity.

shared_paths.rationale: MACOS-WBS extends the audio HAL source enum; other families register their capture backends through this enum in dependency order.

serialized_writers.purpose: non-blocking 60fps soak rebasing on prior writers

shared_paths.rationale: Device probe enumeration is owned by MACOS-WBS; other families extend the enumeration additively.
Playback service is structurally cross-cutting; CTRL-03 (#456) owns the single-voice invariant which all later writers must preserve.
Top-level config schema is structurally cross-cutting; each family appends its own struct/field additively in dependency order. CFG-01 (#482) and HC epic (#385) are the long-term stewards.
shared_paths.merge_rule: Each writer may only ADD fields (and their defaults/serde annotations). Removing or renaming a field claimed by an earlier writer requires a tentacle-level decision.

shared_paths.rationale: CTRL family owns the live-controls TUI surface; UX/I18N/SUPERTONIC layer on top.
UX-01 (#479) owns adaptive layout writes; QA8-06 (#504) reads telemetry without modifying.
Provider trait module is cross-cutting; MODEL-01 (#457) defines the backend contract first, then CTRL-02 (#455) and SUPERTONIC-06 (#491) extend the TTS surface in order.
SUPERTONIC-08 (#493) creates this subtree; subsequent SUPERTONIC issues edit inside it.

shared_paths.merge_rule: Additive only — issues may APPEND new entries under [dependencies], [features], or [target.'cfg(...)'.dependencies]. Removing or pinning a dependency claimed by another family requires a tentacle-level decision. Conflict resolved by lockfile (--locked) gate from CI-01 (#461).

shared_paths.rationale: CI matrix file edited by CI/MODEL/SEC/LINUX/QA8 families; serialization is by R4-documented dependency chain (CI-01 first).

collision_resolution.src/audio/router.rs: MACOS-WBS owns (new file via 453); LINUX-04 (#471) extends after.
collision_resolution.src/pipeline/playback.rs: Shared; serialized 456→454→455→453→495 with CTRL-03's single-voice invariant preserved by all later writers.
collision_resolution.src/config/mod.rs: Shared additive; serialized 452→454→456→455→457→481→482→491→492→496 with field-add-only merge rule.
collision_resolution.src/tui/frame_pacer.rs: UX owns writes (479); QA8-06 (504) is read-only.
collision_resolution.Cargo.toml: Manifest-shared; additive-only merge rule; --locked CI gate from #461 enforces lockfile.

non_owning_issues.rationale: Issues whose snapshot files_touched_hint contained ONLY broad globs (src/audio/**, src/providers/**, src/**) with no R4-audited concrete paths are classified as planning-stage. They do not establish file ownership in this map; concrete ownership will be assigned when they spawn WBS children or when their implementation spike resolves.

readiness_caveats: Serialization rules for cross-family shared src/** paths are derived by R5 from R4 dependency annotations PLUS architectural reasoning. Where R4 did not record an explicit cross-family dependency (e.g., CTRL ↔ MACOS-WBS on src/audio/mod.rs, CTRL ↔ MODEL on src/providers/mod.rs and src/config/mod.rs), R5 has assigned an owner family and a sequenced writer list; these rules must be ratified by the orchestrator before parallel implementation tentacles are dispatched.

Issues classified as planning-stage (the 18 in non_owning_issues) MUST spawn WBS children with concrete files_touched_hint values before they can be assigned to implementation tentacles; otherwise the map will fragment into late collisions.

Cargo.toml uses an additive merge rule rather than serialization; this is safe because the --locked CI gate (#461) prevents silent dependency drift, but the rule cannot detect semver upgrades pushed by two families simultaneously. If two families bump the same dependency in overlapping windows, the later-merged PR must rerun the earlier family's tests.
src/tui/frame_pacer.rs has a writer-reader split (UX-01 writes, QA8-06 reads). If QA8-06 ever needs to write the file, it must be rebased onto UX-01 explicitly.
STD-02 (#484) is intentionally last-in-line and will refactor multiple files; the orchestrator must hold STD-02 until all other src-touching tentacles for the affected files have merged.
notes: No issue bodies, labels, comments, project state, or src/** code were modified. Read-only research artifact.

Per-issue owned_paths under verification-evidence/ for WP-19.02..09 (#115–#122) use the rc-tag placeholder {RC_TAG} per the R4 audit's evidence anchor; concrete tag is filled in when a release candidate is cut.
DM family (#376/#384) currently contains only DM-08 (#384) on the board; DM-01..07 are not on the Todo column and are out of scope for this R5 map.

WP-22/WP-23 (Azure/Ollama) deferred epics list src/providers/azure/ and src/providers/ollama/ as future subtrees; these directories do not exist today and will be created cleanly by future implementation issues. No current collision.
schema_version 1 is the initial R5 schema; downstream consumers (implementation dispatchers, branch-protection helpers) should refuse to consume a higher schema_version they don't understand.
